not_run_validations: npm install (skipped to avoid full frontend build); npm test; npm run rsn

fixes.fix_rationale: express-jwt < 5.3.3 (GHSA-6cwn-77pq-3fpx) does not enforce algorithms; jsonwebtoken < 9 has multiple critical CVEs including signature-bypass. Upgrading to 8.x/9.x eliminates the alg:none family and forces explicit algorithm pinning.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Public API of these libs changed between major versions. Companion SAST branch (remediation/sast-20260512-022728) rewires lib/insecurity.ts to use the new express-jwt({ secret, algorithms:['RS256'] }) and jwt.verify(token, key, { algorithms:['RS256'] }) signatures. SCA branch MUST be merged in tandem with the SAST branch (or after it) so that the build remains green.

validation.notes: Reviewers MUST run `npm install` and `npm run test:server` after merging together with SAST PR. Frontend build runs via postinstall and was not executed here to keep this PR's scope to lockfile-impact only. No lockfile is committed in this repo (gitignored).

fixes.residual_risk: If merged without the corresponding SAST changes to lib/insecurity.ts, the server will fail to compile because the express-jwt v8 default export and constructor signature differs. See PR body for sequencing.

fixes.fix_rationale: multer < 2.1.1 has 6+ DoS advisories (CVE-2026-3520 et al.) that crash the Node process on crafted multipart requests. Bumping to ^2.0.2 (latest 2.x line currently published) closes those advisories. The middleware API used by Juice Shop (`multer({ storage, limits })`, `.single()`/`.array()`) is unchanged between 1.x-lts and 2.x.
fixes.functionality_preservation: All multer call sites in server.ts use the documented public API which is stable across the upgrade. No code changes required.

validation.notes: Reviewers should run the file-upload Frisby suites (test/api/fileUploadSpec.ts, test/api/profileImageUploadSpec.ts) to confirm parity.

fixes.residual_risk: Low. multer 2.x adds stricter validation; legacy clients relying on undocumented quirks may need adjustment.

fixes.fix_rationale: sanitize-html 1.4.2 has 7 high-severity advisories: XSS bypass via mixed-case tags, ReDoS in URL parsing, IDN bypass. The 2.13+ line addresses all of them while keeping the `sanitizeHtml(html)` call signature compatible with how lib/insecurity.ts uses it.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Default tag/attribute allowlist became stricter in 2.x. lib/insecurity.ts only calls `sanitizeHtmlLib(html)` with default options, which is still supported. If existing stored content relied on now-stripped tags, reviewers should validate UI rendering of stored feedback/usernames after the upgrade.

validation.notes: Recommend visual smoke test of pages that render user content (feedback list, reviews, usernames).

fixes.residual_risk: Behavioural drift in HTML stripping for legacy stored content.

fixes.fix_rationale: socket.io 3.x has an unhandled-error-event crash advisory (GHSA-cqmj-92xf-r6r9). The 4.x line addresses it and adds robust error handling defaults. Server and client are upgraded together to keep wire-protocol compatibility.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Server-side usage in lib/startup/registerWebsocketEvents.ts and lib/startup/registerWebsocket*.ts uses the basic `io.on('connection', ...)` and `socket.emit/on` API which is unchanged in 4.x. Default transports (polling+websocket) and CORS auto-handling persist. Reviewers should ensure browser clients are loaded from the same socket.io-client major.

validation.notes: Cypress E2E suite covers chatbot and challenge solve websocket events; recommended.

fixes.residual_risk: Frontend clients pinned to socket.io-client 3 (via frontend/package.json) would lose compatibility. The frontend/package.json was inspected and does not pin socket.io-client; the server-issued client is served via /socket.io/socket.io.js. Smoke test recommended.

fixes.fix_rationale: Align type definitions with the runtime jsonwebtoken@9 upgrade so TypeScript compilation succeeds. Pure devDependency change.

validation.notes: Validated alongside SAST branch's lib/insecurity.ts rewrite.

fixes.fix_rationale: marsdb is unmaintained and has no patched version available — the $where -> new Function() command-injection vector cannot be fixed by upgrade. The companion SAST branch removes all uses of $where on user input (routes/trackOrder.ts, routes/showProductReviews.ts) so the practical RCE/SQLi vector is closed, but the library itself remains in node_modules and continues to be loaded.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Replacing marsdb with mongo-mock / nedb / sequelize for reviews+orders is a multi-day migration: data/mongodb.ts, routes/trackOrder.ts, routes/showProductReviews.ts, routes/updateProductReviews.ts, routes/likeProductReviews.ts, routes/createProductReviews.ts, routes/orderHistory.ts and the data-seed code in data/datacreator.ts all touch it. Out of scope for an automated remediation.
fixes.residual_risk: MEDIUM after SAST fixes are applied (no $where usage on tainted input remains). HIGH if SAST is not merged. Recommend a tracked refactor task to migrate review/order persistence to Sequelize.

fixes.fix_rationale: file-type 17+ is pure ESM. Juice Shop's server is CommonJS (`tsc` to CJS, `require()` semantics through ts-node). Upgrading to 21.3.1 requires changing the import pattern to dynamic `await import('file-type')` AND moving any call sites to async contexts. Doable but interleaves with code changes; safer to handle on a focused dev task.
fixes.functionality_preservation: An ESM->CJS migration would require touching every importer of file-type. Out of scope for an automated dep bump.

validation.notes: Recommended path: add `"type":"module"` or convert file-type call sites to async dynamic import. Track separately.

fixes.residual_risk: MEDIUM (DoS on crafted ASF header). Mitigation in code: feed user buffers through a size+timeout-bounded worker before passing to fileType.fromBuffer.

not_fixed_findings_in_other_categories: All SAST findings (JS-AUDIT-001..005, 007..040, 047) are handled on the companion SAST branch remediation/sast-20260512-022728.
follow_up_actions_for_developers: Run `npm install` locally and commit any project-internal scripts needed for CI.
Run the full `npm test`, `npm run frisby`, and `npm run cypress:run` suites before merging.
Run `npm run rsn` after the SAST branch is merged to update the Refactoring Safety Net snapshots, since routes/login.ts, routes/search.ts, routes/userProfile.ts etc. are challenge-related files.
Plan ESM-compatible upgrade of file-type to >=21.3.1 (JS-AUDIT-045).
Rotate any production JWT/HMAC secrets and revoke previously-issued tokens (covered in SAST manifest).
